extracted.title: TE Pipeline Conditions for 2/10/2026 -- INTRADAY
extracted.description:
For Gas Day February 10, 2026, Texas Eastern (TE) has approved and scheduled nominations at each pipeline segment and meter station up to TE's operational capacity. The following locations have been restricted due to requested nominations exceeding TE's operational capacity:

Restricted Locations include: North of Berne; Meter 75919 Deliveries; Leidy Line Receipts; Armaugh Uncollapsed; Shermansdale/Chambersburg; Grantville/East of Eagle; Lambertville; Linden; Chester Junction 1A1; Chester Junction 1A; TE/AGT Lease (To Lease Capacity); Nexus/TE Leased Meters (To Lease Capacity); 30" South of Berne; Berne South; Danville (Planned Outage); Tompkinsville; Meter 73730 Deliveries; 24"; Sarahsville South; Five Points South; Lebanon; Lebanon Lateral; Batesville South; Deliveries to Meter 74215; Access Area; New Roads South; White Castle South; West Monroe Receipts; Longview; Deliveries to the Castor Line; Speaks; Angleton West (Planned Outage); Deliveries to BIG; East Golden Pass; West Gillis 1; Vidor East; Receipts at Meter 73979.

TABS Pool Restrictions - Tolerances: ETX, STX, WLA, ELA, M1-24", M1-30", M2-24", M2-30", M3. TABS Pool - Length: 1% or 1,000. TABS Pool - Short: 1% or 1,000.

OFO / Imbalance Notice: OFO - No DP - M2-24, M2-30 and M3 effective 2/6/26. 250 Dth / 2% Tolerance. Imbalance Warnings: Stay Balanced - STX, ETX, WLA, ELA, M1-24 and M1-30. FERC Order 698: M2-24, M2-30 and M3.

Shippers in Texas Eastern's Access Area Zones ETX, STX, ELA and WLA as well as Market Area Zones M1-24 and M1-30 are required to coordinate any imbalance payback through TE's account representative and actual payback quantities may be limited depending on system operations and conditions. If customers do not adhere to imbalance requirements stated above, TE will utilize any provision of its tariff to ensure system integrity including the issuance of customer specific or system wide OFOs.

Operational advisory for shippers nominating from 79503: TETLP will upper limit to -0- certain activities if shippers (1) have initial nominations originating from 79503 (WLA TABS Pool) for delivery west of EGP at the Timely Cycle and (2) these shippers are consistently reducing these quantities intraday. TE will be reviewing these activities at the Timely Cycle beginning Gas Day January 30, 2026 until further notice.

(1) Beginning Gas Day December 4, 2025 TETCO will begin restricting delivery nominations at MR 74215 - TEXAS GAS TRANSMISSION LLC - LEBANON, OH to the designed meter capacity of 250,000 Dth/d.

Due to gas quality issues, meter 76609 has been shut in and physical receipts at the meter will be unavailable until further notice. Beginning during the Timely cycle for Gas Day December 6, 2025, and until further notice, TE will restrict receipt nominations at Meter 76609 to -0- Dth/d.

TETLP is monitoring nominations through Gillis and Opelousas and may need to make restrictions in the event the system is at its operational capacity at these stations.

(2) Due to interconnecting pressures at this meter, Tennessee Gas is currently unable to receive gas at this interconnect. TE will schedule meter 73730 to -0- beginning Gas Day February 1, 2026 until further notice.

Due to current TETLP system operating conditions and limited capacity on lateral 15-H, TE is scheduling receipt nominations to approximately 220,000 Dth/d at meter 75660 - UGI APPALACHIA -DRY RIDGE-GREENE CO.,PA(D)73660/(R)73661 until further notice. TETLP will continue to monitor system operations and nominated receipt volumes for future gas days.

Due to gas quality issues, meter 75973 will be scheduled to -0- until further notice.

If customers/point operators do not manage hourly takes from the system, it will lead to delivery pressures being lower than desired for this area. As a result, TE may be required to impose further restrictions or courses of action in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system, including but not limited to, implementation of flow control at specific delivery meter locations.

TE may be required to issue hourly OFO's to impose further restrictions in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system.

Please contact your Operations Account Representative should you have any questions.
